articles.title: Text Classification Using Graph Neural Networks: A Comparative Analysis
articles.authors: Wang K; Ding Y; Han SC
articles.keywords: GNN; text classification; NLP; graph construction; node features; transfer learning
articles.abstract: This paper presents a comparative analysis of graph-based text representation methods combined with GNN architectures for text classification. Twelve GNN architecture variants are evaluated using 3 message-passing layers and 4 pretrained word embedding techniques. Five prominent text classification datasets spanning diverse domains and including both short and longer documents are used. The study investigates efficacy versus efficiency trade-offs, an aspect neglected in prior work. Results show that graph methods generalize variably across datasets, and that ECAP-like cropping strategies improve handling of rare and long-tail classes.
